message:
harness: stable conformance runner + replacement_guard reads from profile

Harness + harness-test side updates that follow from the fixture
schema refactor and from promoting the conformance matrix runner to
a stable entry point.

1. `src/bin/harness.rs` — the `isolate`-mode conformance matrix
   dispatch used `std::env::current_exe()` as the per-case runner
   executable. That works but it's brittle: the current exe path on
   a developer's machine can point to a debug build under
   `target/debug/deps/harness-<hash>`, which is then invoked once
   per case and has to re-parse its own args. A new helper
   `stable_conformance_case_runner(current_exe, output)` resolves
   the current-exe path into a stable, case-runner-shaped path
   (canonicalized, with the output dir available so per-case
   reports land in the same tree). The conformance matrix builder
   now calls `stable_conformance_case_runner` up front and passes
   the resolved path instead.

2. `src/proof_chain_e2e.rs` — small touch-ups that mirror the
   fixture-schema rename (the proof chain walks fixture metadata
   and now has to accept the broader expected-* contract set).

3. `tests/replacement_guard_test.rs::raw_syscalls_are_not_flagged`
   — the test used to parse every .rs file under
   `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src` manually, counting `libc::syscall(`
   occurrences and asserting they came from an allow-listed set
   of known-safe usage sites. That's fragile: every new ABI file
   had to be re-audited and the test's internal allowlist updated
   in lockstep.

   Replace the file-walk with a JSON-driven check that reads the
   existing `replacement_profile.json`'s
   `detection_rules.safe_patterns` array, hydrates it into a
   `HashSet<String>`, and lets the authoritative profile be the
   source of truth. The test still fails if a new `libc::syscall(`
   appears that doesn't match any safe pattern — it just no longer
   owns its own opinion about what "safe" means, which now lives
   exactly once in the profile JSON.

4. `tests/elimination_backoff_artifacts_test.rs`,
   `tests/errno_edge_report_test.rs`,
   `tests/feature_parity_drift_gate_test.rs`,
   `tests/stub_regression_guard_test.rs`,
   `tests/symbol_drift_test.rs` — each gets small adjustments to
   accept the post-refactor JSON report shapes (new field names,
   slightly different path fields, etc.). Nothing asserts weaker
   invariants; the assertions simply follow the new schema.

Ran `cargo build -p frankenlibc-harness` and `cargo test
-p frankenlibc-harness --tests --no-run` after the change to make
sure every test file still compiles. No runtime behaviour changed
in production code paths — this is strictly harness and
harness-adjacent test hygiene.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
